description: reference point: Karpathy GPT baseline (SDPA fallback) from autoresearch harness, same data/tokenizer/budget, seq_len=256, depth=4, aspect_ratio=64, 98.1M tokens, 5989 steps -- run in the separate autoresearch clone, not this repo

exp1: DSP-LM baseline, d_model=256 depth=4 n_states=32 num_branches=8 branch_dim=128, batch=64, lr=3e-4, 55.7M tokens, 3398 steps -- only 2.7/12GB VRAM used, slower per-step than GPT (fft+dendrite overhead not amortized at small batch)

exp2: same arch as exp1, batch 64->128 (device), total_batch 16K->32K to use spare VRAM. WORSE: per-step latency scaled up ~2x with batch (compute-bound, not fixed-overhead-bound at this size), so fewer total tokens (51.9M vs 55.7M) in the same 300s -- reverting batch size next.

exp3: batch back to 64, n_states 32->16 and branch_dim 128->64 (3.7M params vs 4.8M) to fit more steps in budget. WORSE than exp1: capacity loss outweighed the extra steps (3274 steps/53.6M tokens vs exp1's 3398/55.7M -- barely more steps than exp1 despite smaller model, FFT overhead doesn't scale down as much as expected). exp1's config remains best of the three DSP-LM attempts.
